title: Allow multiple paths where the container storage can be mounted in the guest
body:
### Feature request description

I'm experimenting with podman remote to control podman running inside a VM and I'd like to use a unique container storage. In this way, I can pull the image on the host and share the container storage with the VM using virtiofs and run privileged operations inside the guest. 

Everything works nicely except that the container storage path in the guest doesn't match the one in the host. In particularly:
host:  `$HOME/.local/share/containers`
guest: `/var/lib/container` 

When I start using podman inside the guest I get this error message: `Error: database static dir "/home/afrosi/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod" does not match our static dir "/usr/lib/bootc/containers/storage/libpod": database configuration mismatch`

If I delete the ds.sql then the command starts working in the guest but it is, of course now broken on the host. Would be possible to allow multiple paths? Is there a restriction to allow a single path?

### Suggest potential solution

Allow multiple paths in the database

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Possible to use a network protocol to share the directory, but it is some how less efficient

### Additional context

This work is done in the context of bootc for performing operations on block devices while using rootless podman

comments.body:
If you just want to share images you likely want to look at additional image stores which only contains the images and not other things such as the podman db.

Also the image store depends on file locks, is virtiofs preserving them properly, otherwise you could end up corrupting the store easily.

Sharing the full store like that makes no sense (even if we remove the check) because the db stores the container config and state which means a container created in the VM would be shown by podman ps on the host which is not right and most likely results in a ton of errors and undefined behaviours.
@Luap99 can you please share the option? Is this `--storage-opt=additionalimagestores`?
Anyway, my main reason to use a single store is that otherwise I encounter some ostree issue which cannot find an image layer

> When I start using podman inside the guest I get this error message: Error: database static dir "/home/afrosi/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod" does not match our static dir "/usr/lib/bootc/containers/storage/libpod": database configuration mismatch

Yep, see https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23447

> If you just want to share images you likely want to look at additional image stores which only contains the images 

Yes, also discussion on this in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2617

The trick was to mount the /var/lib/containers and the shared directory for the storage into the bootc container in order to correctly find the containers and images. Something like this in my case:
```
	-v /var/lib/containers:/var/lib/containers \
	-v /usr/lib/bootc/storage:/var/lib/containers/storage \
```
